Scenario.label: intro
Scenario.Description: Find an already-running Chrome instance or launch a new one. Sets {{tabId}} for subsequent steps.

1. LISTWINDOWS target="SYSTEM" → look for Chrome entries in the list. 2. If a Chrome instance is found: note the first tab id as {{tabId}}. 3. If Chrome is NOT found: LAUNCH target="chrome" → Chrome opens with a blank tab. Capture the PAGE:id from the CDP_NEWPAGE response or LISTWINDOWS after launch. After intro, {{tabId}} is a valid tab that can receive CDP_NAVIGATE/READ/etc.

Step.note: list all sessions → filter for Chrome entries → assign {{tabId}}

Scenario.Description: Launch Chrome (opens a new blank tab).
Use when Chrome is definitively not running. LAUNCH opens Chrome with the default new-tab page. Follow with CDP_NEWPAGE or CDP_NAVIGATE on the returned tab id.

Step.note: launches Chrome; returns tab list with at least one blank tab

Scenario.Description: Open a new blank tab. Returns PAGE:id as {{tabId}}.
CDP_NEWPAGE opens a blank tab (about:newtab) via CDP Target.createTarget. Preferred over SENDKEYS {CTRL+T} for reliable PAGE:id return. Reassign {{tabId}} from the response before continuing.

Step.note: opens blank tab via CDP; capture returned PAGE:id as {{tabId}}

Scenario.Description: Navigate tab {{tabId}} to {{url}}.

Uses CDP_NAVIGATE — direct CDP Page.navigate call, cleaner and more reliable than the keyboard scenario ({CTRL+L}+url+{ENTER}). After CDP_NAVIGATE, the tab loads the page; allow a brief wait before READ/CDP_PAGESOURCE. The response includes the final URL (after redirects) and page title.

Step.note: navigate to URL via CDP; waits for load event

Scenario.Description: Read the full visible text of the page (body.innerText).

READ with no parameter returns body.innerText — all visible text content. Useful for: verifying navigation succeeded, extracting plain-text data, checking for error messages, reading search results. For structured data prefer CDP_EXECUTE to return JSON. Does NOT return HTML markup.

Step.note: returns full visible page text

Scenario.Description: Read the text content or value of element {{sel}}.

Step.note: returns element innerText or input.value

Scenario.Description: Focus the input element {{sel}} and type {{text}}.

Uses SENDKEYS with "css:{{sel}}|{{text}}" — this focuses the element then types. Use for: search boxes, login fields, form inputs. To clear first: CDP_EXECUTE "document.querySelector('{{sel}}').value=''" then fill. To submit after typing: append {ENTER} to {{text}} or use scenario "submit-form".

Step.note: focus element and type text

Scenario.Description: Click the element identified by CSS selector {{sel}}.

Step.note: click element by CSS selector

Scenario.Description: Fill text field {{sel}} with {{text}} and submit by pressing Enter.

Combines fill-input + {ENTER} key to submit. For multi-field forms: call fill-input for each field, then click the submit button. Monitor URL or page text change after submit to detect navigation/response.

Step.note: type into field and press Enter to submit

Scenario.Description: Run JavaScript {{jsCode}} in the page via CDP_EXECUTE and return the result.

CDP_EXECUTE runs Runtime.evaluate(code); returns JSON-serialized result. For async operations: wrap in an IIFE: (async () => { return await ...; })() Useful for: extracting structured data, checking element states, scrolling, triggering events, reading cookies/storage, injecting UI interactions. Example: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a')).map(a=>a.href))"

Step.note: Runtime.evaluate in page context; returns JSON-serialized result

Scenario.Description: Capture a full-page screenshot of tab {{tabId}}.

CDP_SCREENSHOT returns a base64-encoded PNG via CDP Page.captureScreenshot. Use for: visual verification, debugging, capturing form states. The result can be decoded and saved to disk via CDP_EXECUTE or external processing.

Step.note: CDP Page.captureScreenshot; returns base64 PNG

Scenario.Description: Get the DOM accessibility snapshot of the current page (CDP_PAGESOURCE).

CDP_PAGESOURCE returns the CDP accessibility / snapshot tree (Page.getAccessibilityTree). Useful for: discovering element roles and names, finding selectors, mapping out interactive elements. Result is a JSON/XML tree; interpret roles and names to identify elements. For raw HTML source: use CDP_EXECUTE with document.documentElement.outerHTML. Depth may be limited — use CDP_EXECUTE for deep inspection of specific subtrees.

Step.note: returns CDP accessibility tree snapshot (Page.getAccessibilityTree)

Scenario.Description: Scroll to the bottom of the page (to load lazy content).

Step.note: scroll to bottom

Scenario.Description: Leave the tab open. No teardown.
No-op teardown. Use when you want the user to see the page.
Close the tab that was used for automation.

Step.note: CDP Target.closeTarget; close the automation tab

Scenario.Description: Executes {{jsCode}} in the browser page. Diagnostic use only.

Step.note: evaluate jsCode; result bound to diagResult

Scenario.Description: Open the browser Bookmarks Manager page and read the visible folder list from the page text. No JS injection needed — the AI reads the rendered bookmark tree as visible text.

KEYBOARD APPROACH: 1. Press Ctrl+Shift+O in the browser window — this opens chrome://bookmarks/ as a new tab. Alternatively navigate the current tab to chrome://bookmarks/. 2. Wait for the page to render (the bookmark manager is a full web page). 3. READ the page — the bookmark manager renders all folder names and link titles as visible text. Parse that text to extract folder names.
Step by step using AIAPI commands: 1. SENDKEYS HANDLE:{{browserHwnd}} "{CTRL+SHIFT+O}" → opens chrome://bookmarks/ tab; note the new PAGE:id from LISTWINDOWS. 2. READ PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}} → returns full visible text: folder names, link titles, URLs. Parse to find top-level folders (lines with no leading indent). 3. Present folder list to user.
NOTE: If the browser was opened with CDP (remote-debugging-port), you may instead use CDP_NAVIGATE to chrome://bookmarks/ on an existing tab, then READ that tab — same result without needing keyboard focus.

Param.name: browserHwnd
Param.note: Window handle of the browser (from LISTWINDOWS)

Param.name: bookmarksTabId
Param.note: PAGE:id of the tab showing chrome://bookmarks/; obtained after Ctrl+Shift+O

Step.note: open Bookmarks Manager as new tab (keyboard shortcut)
find the new PAGE:id for the chrome://bookmarks/ tab; assign to bookmarksTabId
read visible bookmark tree text; AI parses folder names from the output

Scenario.Description: Open Bookmarks Manager, type {{query}} into the search box, and read the matching results from the page.

Pure keyboard approach — types in the Bookmarks Manager search box and reads back the filtered results as visible text.
Step by step: 1. SENDKEYS HANDLE:{{browserHwnd}} "{CTRL+SHIFT+O}" → opens chrome://bookmarks/ with search box already focused. 2. LISTWINDOWS → get PAGE:id for the bookmarks tab ({{bookmarksTabId}}). 3. SENDKEYS PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}} "{{query}}" → types the search query; chrome://bookmarks/ filters results in real time. 4. READ PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}} → returns the filtered result list as visible text (bookmark titles + URLs). AI extracts the bookmark titles and URLs from the text output. 5. Present results to user, or proceed to bookmarks-open.
The chrome://bookmarks/ search box receives keyboard focus automatically when the page opens via Ctrl+Shift+O, so typing immediately filters results.

Param.name: query
Param.note: text to type in the bookmarks search box

Param.name: bookmarksTabId
Param.note: PAGE:id of chrome://bookmarks/ tab (filled in from LISTWINDOWS after step 1)

Step.note: open Bookmarks Manager; search box auto-focuses
get PAGE:id for the new chrome://bookmarks/ tab
type search query; results filter in real time
read filtered results; AI parses titles and URLs

Scenario.Description: Navigate to Bookmarks Manager, click the folder named {{folderName}}, and read its contents from the rendered page.

Mouse-click approach — finds the named folder in the bookmark tree and clicks it to reveal its contents, then reads the visible result.
Step by step: 1. SENDKEYS HANDLE:{{browserHwnd}} "{CTRL+SHIFT+O}" → opens chrome://bookmarks/. 2. LISTWINDOWS → get PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}}. 3. READ PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}} → scan the text for {{folderName}} to confirm it is visible. 4. CLICKID PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}} css:[title="{{folderName}}"] → clicks the folder row in the bookmark manager tree. Alternative selector if exact title fails: css:bookmark-folder (first match). 5. READ PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}} → page now shows the folder's contents; AI parses the visible items.
If the folder is not visible in the main pane (deep nesting), scroll using SENDKEYS {PAGE_DOWN} or repeat CLICKID on parent folders first.

Param.name: folderName
Param.note: exact or partial folder title visible on screen

Step.note: open Bookmarks Manager
get PAGE:id for the bookmarks tab
read page to confirm folder is visible
click the folder to expand/select it
read the folder contents now shown in the pane

Scenario.Description: Open Bookmarks Manager, search for {{title}}, click the first matching result to navigate to that bookmarked page.

Full keyboard+mouse flow to open a bookmark by name — no JS injection.
1. SENDKEYS HANDLE:{{browserHwnd}} "{CTRL+SHIFT+O}" → opens chrome://bookmarks/; search box receives focus. 2. LISTWINDOWS → get PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}}. 3. SENDKEYS PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}} "{{title}}" → types the bookmark title; results filter in real time. 4. READ PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}} → AI reads the result list, identifies the first URL shown. If no results: report "bookmark not found" to user and stop. 5. CLICKID PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}} css:bookmark-item[role='listitem'] → clicks the first result row; this navigates the tab to the bookmarked page (chrome://bookmarks/ replaces itself with the URL).
After step 5 the tab is now showing the bookmarked page. Confirm with READ PAGE:{{bookmarksTabId}} to verify the title/URL.
ALTERNATIVE when URL is already known: a. SENDKEYS HANDLE:{{browserHwnd}} "{CTRL+L}" (focus address bar) b. SENDKEYS HANDLE:{{browserHwnd}} "{{url}}{ENTER}" (type URL + Enter) This skips the bookmark manager entirely and navigates directly.

Param.name: title
Param.note: text to type in the bookmarks search box

Param.name: bookmarksTabId
Param.note: PAGE:id of chrome://bookmarks/ tab (filled after LISTWINDOWS)

Step.note: open Bookmarks Manager; search box auto-focuses
get PAGE:id for chrome://bookmarks/ tab
type bookmark title to filter results
verify search results; AI reads the first matching title and URL
click first result; tab navigates to the bookmarked URL

Scenario.label: detect-locale
Scenario.Description: Read the browser’s active locale (BCP-47) and bind it to {{appLocale}}.
Execute navigator.language via CDP and capture the result into {{appLocale}}. Use before any LocaleMap lookup: GET /api/appTemplates/{app}/scenarios/{id}/localeMap?lang={{appLocale}}

Param.name: tabId
Param.note: CDP page target handle; auto-bound by intro scenario

Step.note: returns BCP-47 tag e.g. en-US; stored in {{appLocale}}

Scenario.Description: Navigate to about:blank (clear page state without closing tab).

Step.note: navigates to blank page via CDP; tab remains open
